terms.definition: A standard-library module providing the object-oriented Path type for filesystem paths and file operations.
A pathlib.Path instance representing a filesystem path, with properties (name, stem, suffix, parent) and methods (exists, read_text, glob).
A named value provided by the operating system or container to a process, read in Python via os.environ; commonly used for config and secrets.
Matching files by a wildcard pattern; Path.glob matches one directory level and Path.rglob matches recursively.
The directory a process is currently running in (os.getcwd()); relative paths are resolved against it.
